text.content: (1) Add a unit_drops audit view `pds.supplement_intake_unmapped` = SELECT product_id, ingredient_group, label_unit, COUNT(*) FROM supplement_intake JOIN supplement_products WHERE unit_to_mg_factor(label_unit) IS NULL GROUP BY 1,2,3 -- expose on /status as a drop counter. (2) Add a (unii_code, source_unit, target_unit, factor) lookup table so IU can convert per-vitamin. (3) For volumetric units (mL, drops, oz), require label density in the product seed so the conversion can be (volume * density_mg_per_ml). (4) Log NULL returns to sync_log.

rich_text.plain_text: (1) Add a unit_drops audit view `pds.supplement_intake_unmapped` = SELECT product_id, ingredient_group, label_unit, COUNT(*) FROM supplement_intake JOIN supplement_products WHERE unit_to_mg_factor(label_unit) IS NULL GROUP BY 1,2,3 -- expose on /status as a drop counter. (2) Add a (unii_code, source_unit, target_unit, factor) lookup table so IU can convert per-vitamin. (3) For volumetric units (mL, drops, oz), require label density in the product seed so the conversion can be (volume * density_mg_per_ml). (4) Log NULL returns to sync_log.

text.content: Audit the definitions of the `pds.daily_health_matrix_behavioral` and `pds.recovery_vs_pace` views. Ensure that any HRV column has a single, well-defined source (e.g., it is always WHOOP RMSSD). If a unified HRV metric is desired, it must be created with a statistically sound method (e.g., z-scoring each source independently before combining), not by direct averaging. Rename columns in the views to be specific (e.g., `whoop_hrv_rmssd_ms`) to prevent future confusion.

rich_text.plain_text: Audit the definitions of the `pds.daily_health_matrix_behavioral` and `pds.recovery_vs_pace` views. Ensure that any HRV column has a single, well-defined source (e.g., it is always WHOOP RMSSD). If a unified HRV metric is desired, it must be created with a statistically sound method (e.g., z-scoring each source independently before combining), not by direct averaging. Rename columns in the views to be specific (e.g., `whoop_hrv_rmssd_ms`) to prevent future confusion.

text.content: 1. Query the production `supplement_products` table to identify all distinct units currently in use. 2. Extend the `pds.unit_to_mg_factor` function to support these units. For units like 'IU' that require a substance-specific conversion factor, this may require a more complex function or a separate lookup table (`compound_to_mg_factor`). 3. For non-convertible units ('scoops', 'capsules'), decide on a handling strategy (e.g., log a warning, store dosage as a string). 4. Add monitoring to detect and alert when a new, unrecognized unit is introduced.

rich_text.plain_text: 1. Query the production `supplement_products` table to identify all distinct units currently in use. 2. Extend the `pds.unit_to_mg_factor` function to support these units. For units like 'IU' that require a substance-specific conversion factor, this may require a more complex function or a separate lookup table (`compound_to_mg_factor`). 3. For non-convertible units ('scoops', 'capsules'), decide on a handling strategy (e.g., log a warning, store dosage as a string). 4. Add monitoring to detect and alert when a new, unrecognized unit is introduced.

text.content: Add coverage for common units and introduce explicit fallbacks: (a) Extend dictionary for volume (mL, L) with concentration-aware paths when per-serving mass is available; (b) Handle IU via compound-specific mappings (e.g., Vitamin D: 1 IU = 0.025 µg cholecalciferol) by moving conversion to a function that accepts (compound_name, unit) or by joining to a per-compound unit map; (c) For truly unconvertible units, emit a sentinel (e.g., 0 with flagged column) rather than NULL and add a log/metric of unknown-unit counts so rows aren’t silently dropped.

rich_text.plain_text: Add coverage for common units and introduce explicit fallbacks: (a) Extend dictionary for volume (mL, L) with concentration-aware paths when per-serving mass is available; (b) Handle IU via compound-specific mappings (e.g., Vitamin D: 1 IU = 0.025 µg cholecalciferol) by moving conversion to a function that accepts (compound_name, unit) or by joining to a per-compound unit map; (c) For truly unconvertible units, emit a sentinel (e.g., 0 with flagged column) rather than NULL and add a log/metric of unknown-unit counts so rows aren’t silently dropped.

text.content: Switch to TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=5) for the cross-fitting splits, which preserves time order. As a check, add an option to compute a block-bootstrap CI (block length ~7-14 days) on the AIPW ATE alongside the IF-based one and compare; if they diverge meaningfully, the IF SE was too narrow.
